hints.junior: Before starting any LCP optimization, run the PerformanceObserver snippet to see what element the browser actually flagged. It's often not what you think.
hints.mid: The filmstrip in Chrome DevTools Performance panel shows a yellow marker for LCP. Hover it: the highlighted element is your target. Start there, not with the hero image.
hints.staff: CrUX LCP attribution is more valuable than lab data here; real users on real devices may have a different LCP element than your dev machine shows.

hints.junior: The broken demo simulates a page waiting for a font before the heading renders; that wait is measured as LCP time. The fixed version preloads the font so the heading renders immediately.
hints.mid: The LCP score difference between the two modes is typically 1-2 seconds on Slow 3G, the difference between 'Good' and 'Needs Improvement' in CWV.
hints.staff: The four-phase LCP breakdown (TTFB + load delay + load time + render delay) is in PageSpeed Insights under 'Diagnose performance issues'. Address the largest phase first.

hints.junior: The concept chapter is the principle: read it before the demo so the toggle makes sense.
hints.mid: Connect the principle to your component API. What prop or CSS change encodes the fix?
hints.staff: Map the pattern to observability: what would you measure in RUM or lab to prove the fix?

hints.junior: Skim the code patterns here; you can copy the structure even if the syntax is new.
hints.mid: Compare broken vs fixed implementation. The diff is usually smaller than you expect.
hints.staff: Check edge cases: SSR, hydration, design tokens, and high-contrast mode.

anecdotes.junior: I spent a week compressing images for a marketing site. LCP didn't budge because the actual LCP element was an h1 waiting on a 350KB font file. Preloading the font fixed it.
anecdotes.mid: Every performance sprint I've seen assume LCP = hero image. The PerformanceObserver check takes 30 seconds; add it to your perf audit checklist first.
anecdotes.staff: For a product with text-heavy above-fold content (news, docs, SaaS dashboards), text LCP is nearly always the case. Image-focused optimization is often a red herring.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're solving the mystery of LCP: why optimizing the hero image doesn't always help, and how to find the actual element that's slowing your score.
sections.mid: This case covers LCP element identification: why it's often text, how to measure it with PerformanceObserver, and the right optimization for each element type.
sections.staff: We're covering LCP attribution phases in CrUX, font-loading impact on text LCP, and SSR as the fastest path to low LCP for text elements.

sections.staff: Map the pattern to observability: what would you measure in RUM or lab to prove the fix?

sections.staff: The four-phase LCP breakdown (TTFB + load delay + load time + render delay) is in PageSpeed Insights under 'Diagnose performance issues'. Address the largest phase first.
